entries.s: Please close the window — it is very _____ today. My fingers are turning blue!
Mum is _____ because I broke her favourite cup. She is shouting at me.
The little boy is _____ of the big loud dog. He is crying and hiding behind his mother.

entries.d: feeling very happy about something coming
entries.s: Tomorrow is Christmas morning! The children are so _____ they can't sleep.
In summer it is very _____ outside. The sun is burning and everyone is sweating.
There is nothing fun to do here. I am so _____ that I am just staring at the wall.
Her pet cat is lost and she feels very _____. Tears are running down her face.
I did not have any breakfast this morning, and now my stomach is making loud noises. I am _____.
He has a big maths test today and he is _____. His hands are shaking.
It is very late at night and the baby is so _____ that her eyes are closing.

entries.d: feeling good about something you did
entries.s: She won first place in the race and her dad is very _____. He is telling everyone about it.

entries.d: the early part of the day
entries.s: I eat cereal and toast for breakfast every _____ before I go to school at 8 am.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with MORNING?

entries.d: the time when you eat lunch
entries.s: At _____, which is 12 o'clock, we go to the canteen and eat our sandwiches.

entries.d: the time between lunch and evening
entries.s: School finishes at three o'clock in the _____, after the lunch break.

entries.d: the time after afternoon and before night
entries.s: We eat dinner together as a family at 7 o'clock in the _____ after work.

entries.d: the dark time when you sleep
entries.s: The stars come out and the sky is dark at _____ when everyone is sleeping.

entries.d: the first day of the week
entries.s: The weekend is over. Today is _____.

entries.d: the second day of the week
entries.s: Yesterday was Monday, so today is _____.

entries.d: the third day of the week
entries.s: It is the middle of the week. Today is _____.

entries.d: the fourth day of the week
entries.s: Tomorrow is Friday, so today is _____.

entries.d: the fifth day of the week
entries.s: Tomorrow is Saturday! Today is _____.

entries.d: the sixth day, first weekend day
entries.s: There is no school today because it is _____.

entries.d: the seventh day of the week
entries.s: We always visit grandma on _____.
We always play football _____ school.
What _____ is it today? It is Monday.
At the _____ of the film, the credits start rolling and everyone claps.
Playing board games with my friends on a rainy day is great _____ for all of us.
I don't want to talk to you right now. _____ and leave me alone!
What is _____ over there in the corner? I can hear shouting and people running.

entries.d: a performance for people to watch
entries.s: We are going to watch a live _____ at the theatre with music, dancing, and actors.

a moment in the life of

entries.s: Be very quiet! The baby is _____ in her cot, with her eyes closed.

entries.d: near the start of the day
entries.s: Dad gets up at 5am, very _____. It is still dark outside and everyone else is asleep.

entries.d: the time when you are alive
entries.s: My grandma tells long stories about her childhood and her whole _____ in the village 80 years ago.
It is ten o'clock in the morning and he is _____ in bed sleeping. He is so lazy!

entries.d: something you read or someone tells you
entries.s: Mum reads me a bedtime _____ with princes and dragons before I go to sleep.
_____ is my tenth birthday! I am going to have a party later.
You look very sad and your eyes are red. _____ Please tell me what is wrong.

entries.d: school work you do at home
entries.s: I must do my maths _____ assigned by the teacher before I can play outside.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with HOMEWORK?

entries.s: The curious cat jumped _____ the big cardboard box and hid inside.

entries.d: you say this when something is wrong
entries.s: _____ I forgot my expensive school bag on the bus this morning and now it is lost!

entries.d: a part of a house or building
entries.s: My little sister and I share a small bedroom _____ with two beds and one wardrobe.
The weather is very _____ today. It is raining hard and the wind is blowing.

entries.d: telling someone to come on time
entries.s: The school bus leaves at exactly eight o'clock. _____ Or you will miss it!
Today is Monday, so _____ — the day after — is Tuesday.

entries.d: the day you were born, celebrated yearly
entries.s: I get lots of presents and a chocolate cake with candles on my _____ every year.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with BIRTHDAY?

entries.d: a person you like and play with
entries.s: Tom is my best _____. We do homework together and play at each other's houses.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with FRIEND?

entries.s: Don't try to copy someone else at the party. _____ People will like the real you.
Be yourself and be sure that _____ can be a better you than you can.
Can I have a plastic _____ of cold water, please? I am very thirsty.

entries.d: to have something returned to you
entries.s: I lent Anna my favourite book last week. I want to _____ it from her today.
Dad is very _____ because my football broke his favourite window. He is shouting at me.
The wizard's _____ wand can make things disappear and reappear in a puff of smoke.

entries.d: to make something go into pieces
entries.s: Be careful with that glass cup! Don't drop it and _____ it into pieces.

entries.d: to close your eyes and rest at night
entries.s: It is past bedtime and you have school tomorrow. You must _____ right now in your bed.
I am very happy today _____ it is the start of the weekend and I have no school.
Who is knocking at the door? — _____ Open the door, it's Anna from next door!

entries.d: telling someone to do something new
entries.s: This chocolate birthday cake is delicious — the best I've ever eaten. _____ You'll love it!
You are holding my arm too tight and it hurts! _____ Stop pulling me!
I can hear a loud noise and shouting from upstairs. _____ Is everything OK?
